title: Why I Travel The World Alone
creator: Bashir Abu Shakra
award_or_showcase_provenance: Drone Photo Awards (Siena Drone Photo Awards, droneawards.photo) 2023 edition -- Pangea Prize, Video category, 1st classified winner ("Video: General" sub-section of the site's own gallery)
notes: MANUALLY CURATED WORKED EXAMPLE -- one exemplar only, per spec AC3.4/Scope-out (no automated scraping/bulk downloading was performed to produce this record). ASSIGNMENT: a winner from Drone Awards, Siena Drone Photo Award, or a comparably established international drone photography/video competition, in a landscape or wildlife category not already covered by this batch's other exemplars. Verified this session via YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Z6t4y3A28uA&format=json), which returned the exact title 'Why I Travel The World Alone 4k' and channel/author 'Bashir Abu Shakra' (https://www.youtube.com/@BashirAbuShakra) for this URL -- a verbatim match (modulo the '4k' suffix) to the creator/title pairing found on the awards site itself. This session fetched droneawards.photo/gallery/2023/video directly via a cookie-jar curl session (the same technique used for this pack's existing 'drone-photography-awards-wildlife.json' Botswana record, since the site issues session cookies and otherwise redirect-loops) and confirmed the raw HTML contains, under the page's 'Video: General' sub-heading, a block reading '1st classified' / 'Why I Travel The World Alone' / 'by Bashir Abu Shakra' / synopsis '4 years ago, I started my solo-journey to capture the most beautiful and remote landscapes on earth starting from the Alps then Brazil and Mongolia...' embedding YouTube video id Z6t4y3A28uA -- i.e. this exact YouTube URL is the awards site's own embedded video for its 1st-place Video-category entry, not an inferred or third-party reupload. Independent web-search corroboration (diyphotography.net's 'Powerful protest photo wins Siena Drone Photo Awards 2023' coverage, plus other secondary sources) names this same title/creator pairing as winner of the 'Pangea Prize' in the Drone Photo Awards 2023 Video category, and further independently corroborates that the same film separately won 1st place in the 'Dronography' category at the 6th Jeju International Drone Film Festival and the Annual Best Work Prize at DJI/SkyPixel's 8th Annual contest -- multiple independent festival/contest recognitions for the same verified work, well beyond a single arbitrary upload. CATEGORY-ASSIGNMENT NOTE: as with this pack's other Drone-Photo-Awards exemplar (Botswana by Drone), the win is technically in the contest's genre-based 'Video' category (this site does not run a separate subject-based 'Landscape' video category), but the film's own synopsis and press coverage ('sweeping mountaintop landscapes', 'the most beautiful and remote landscapes on earth', shot across the Alps/Brazil/Mongolia) make its landscape subject matter unambiguous and substantially stronger than a mere travel/human-interest piece -- distinct in creator, title, and specific award from every other exemplar already in this pack (including the existing SkyPixel-attributed 'Iceland - Feel The Elements', which this same droneawards.photo 2023 gallery page also happens to list, in a different year/edition, as a 2022 Highly-Commended entry -- not reused here). `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': this session fetched the raw YouTube watch-page HTML for this URL via curl and grepped case-insensitively for 'creative commons' markers, finding none (only generic UI-string occurrences of the substring 'licens' from YouTube's own interface localization bundle) -- so per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo. `duration` is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response does not include a duration field, and no YouTube Data API call was made in this session -- rather than fabricate a plausible-looking number, this field is left unset pending a real API call or manual playback confirmation (see reference_pack/README.md's refresh/expiry process). `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` placeholder judgments based on the film's documented multi-award pedigree (Drone Photo Awards Pangea Prize winner, Jeju International Drone Film Festival 1st place, SkyPixel Annual Best Work Prize -- placing it among the most decorated works considered for this pack), NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the same no-bulk-download constraint. `composite_score` IS a real computation: it was produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.82, exposure=0.80, motion_smoothness=0.84, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` (weights_version 'default-v1', equal 1/3 weighting on sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness, composition weight fixed at 0.0) via `.venv/bin/python`, yielding composite_score=0.8200000000000001 (rounded to 0.82 here) -- so the composite arithmetic itself is genuine and reproducible, even though its inputs are estimates rather than measurements.
